doc.title: Corridor Amortization of Net Gain/Loss
doc.event: At January 1, Brant Co. reports: projected benefit obligation $2,100,000, market-related value of plan assets $1,900,000, and accumulated OCI net loss $260,000. Average remaining service life of active employees is 10 years. Compute the minimum amortization of the net loss included in pension expense under the corridor approach.

computationPaths.narration: The corridor is 10% of the LARGER of beginning PBO or market-related asset value. Only the loss sticking out past the corridor gets amortized — spread over average remaining service life.

memorize.body: Corridor = 10% × larger of beginning PBO or market-related value of plan assets
Use BEGINNING-of-year balances for both the corridor and the accumulated gain/loss test.
Inside the corridor, NOTHING is amortized — a net loss can sit in AOCI indefinitely if it never breaches 10%.

questions.prompt: PBO $2,100,000 · MRV of assets $1,900,000 · AOCI net loss $260,000 · service life 10 years. Minimum amortization = ?

distractors.feedback: You amortized the whole 260,000. Only the excess OVER the corridor (260,000 − 210,000 = 50,000) is amortized.
You amortized the corridor itself (210,000 ÷ 10). The corridor is the shield, not the amount amortized.
You found the right excess but skipped the last step — divide by the 10-year average remaining service life.
